**SGSC-AI Intelligence Probe Executed**

Nanjing Guixin Electronic Technology Co., Ltd. is a sample of systemic misjudgment that can be called 'absurdist magical realism.' The forensic engine has completely wiped out the underlying fake consumer electronics OEM data triggered by Apple Inc. The official commercial system mechanically classified it at the top of the industrial pyramid under 'Computer, Communication and Other Electronic Equipment Manufacturing - Electronic Device Manufacturing,' and endowed it with the volume tag of a 'Small Enterprise,' simply because its name contains 'Electronic Technology' and includes a vague 'technology development.' However, the physical probe and common-sense engine immediately executed a comprehensive peeling: this is an old micro-enterprise established in 2010, with a paid-in capital of only 500,000, and its 2024 insured headcount is astonishingly an absolute '0 people'! Its registered address is located at 'No. 218 Xiaowei Street, Xuanwu District, Nanjing City,' which on a map usually points to an ordinary street-side storefront, an old residential community ground-floor shop, or an urban village. In such geographical coordinates, relying on 0 employees to establish an industrial manufacturing assembly line for electronic devices? This not only violates physical common sense but is simply a humorous deconstruction of heavy industry. Even more laughable is its 'encyclopedic' business license, over a hundred words long and all-encompassing: extending from selling 'electronic products' all the way to 'bedding, cotton textiles, wood products'; businesses cover not only 'indoor and outdoor decoration engineering, water and electricity installation' but even include 'cleaning services' and 'flower leasing'! The diagnostic conclusion is without suspense: this is absolutely not a major electronic manufacturing plant; this is a pure Zero-Employee Universal General Store & Handyman Shell hidden in a street shop on Xiaowei Street. The boss (Xu Jun) highly likely opened a small shop by the street, helping people repair computers (computer maintenance), incidentally selling some groceries and bedding, or taking on odd jobs like clearing sewers, doing cleaning, and arranging potted plants.
